text: The patient is positioned in the supine and Trendelenburg position. Conventional placement of robotic trocars is visible. The surgeon begins by freeing pelvic adhesions, and instillation through the urethral catheter is performed to identify the knee or bladder.
The surgeon dissects the left ureter at both its distal and proximal portions, identifying a short stenosis at the level of the left ureteroenteric anastomosis.
The surgeon performs direct ureteral reimplantation, utilizing an exteriorized ureteral catheter to facilitate the procedure.
The surgeon frees and dissects the right ureter, identifying a stenosis. The ureter is confirmed to reach the bladder with tension.
The surgeon creates a new bladder flap and performs a body-type ureteral reimplantation using robotic assistance.
